content-right-description1:
Overview

Satellites are absolutly essential for the whole game, collecting data of planets.
content-right-description2:
Step 1: Building the launch pad

To build a rocket you obivously need a launch pad, which in our case consists of a at least 3x3 launchpad, a at least 5 blobks high structure tower and a rocket assembler.
To link any launch site equipment use a linker.
Also required is a fueling station to fuel your rocket. Any other equipment is not essential.
content-right-description3:
Step 2: Building the rocket

Every rocket requires fuel tank, rocket engine and a guidance computer. Any other block depends on your usage.
To assemble the rocket, click 'build' in the roccket assembler (remember that there is a 1 block padding).The amount of tanks, or engines is determined by the rockets mass and how much thrust the engines give.
content-right-description4:
Step 3: Launching the rocket

To launch your rocket, it will need to fuel your rocket with rocket fuel.
Launching can eather be done by accesing the rocket guidance computer and inserting the goal location (eather a linker or a planet id chip. Alternatifly you can use a launch station, which will overwrite the location set in the guidance computer.
Click 'launch' to start the rocket.
All the stats of the rocket while flight and before are displayed in the guidance computer and in the rocket assembler.
content-right-description5:
Manned missions

To ride a rocket, place a seat on your rocket, and use a space suit(currently not implemented).
content-right-description6:
Missions

Rockets have way more capabilities then just sending people into space, via a mission launch terminal (asteroid mission launch staion and gas mining mission terminal), for more detail look into the guides.
